title: viola bhutanica
intro: Viola bhutanica, also known as the Himalayan pansy or Bhutan violet, is a rare, alpine plant species belonging to the violet family (Violaceae). It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Viola bhutanica are commonly consumed in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
